LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TWENTIETH CENTURY FOX FILM CORPORATION, LEE DANIELS, MALCOLM SPELLMAN, TERRENCE HOWARD, AND DANNY STRONG'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANTS ARE AWARDED ATTORNEY'S FEES AGAINST PLAINTIFF RON NEWT IN THE AMOUNT OF $48,000. NO OPPOSITION FILED. PER TAMKIN V. CBS BROADCASTING, INC. (2011) 193 CAL.APP. 4TH 133, AT LEAST SEVERAL CAUSES OF ACTION IN MR. NEWT'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ARISE FROM ACTIVITY PROTECTED BY THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE BECAUSE THE CREATION AND AIRING OF THE TELEVISION SHOW EMPIRE CONSTITUTES PROTECTED ACTIVITY UNDER THAT STATUTE. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR $117,156.50 (AMOUNT REQUESTED) IN ATTORNEY'S FEES IS EXCESSIVE GIVEN THE ISSUES INVOLVED IN THIS MOTION AND THEIR ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. WHILE THE COURT DOES NOT QUESTION THAT COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS SPENT THE HOURS THAT THEY SAY THEY SPENT AND THEIR CLIENTS ARE PERMITTED TO HAVE THEM SPEND THOSE HOURS, THE HOURS SPENT FAR EXCEED WHAT WAS REASONABLY NECESSARY TO SUCCEED ON THE MOTIONS. THE COURT CONCLUDES THAT 2 WEEKS OF WORK (80 HOURS) AT AN HOURLY RATE OF $600 IS A REASONABLE AMOUNT OF HOURS TO SPEND ON THESE MOTIONS. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants Twentieth Century Fox Film Corporation Lee Daniels Terrence Howard Malcolm Spellman And Danny Strong For Attorneys Fees Pursuant To Californias Antislapp Statute Code Civ Proc 425.16 C1
Off Calendar Jul-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, Elaine K. Kim and Robert H. Rotstein, each of the law offices of Mitchell Silberberg & Knupp LLP (310.312.3215) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendants Twentieth Century Fox Film Corporation, Lee Daniels, Malcolm Spellman, Terrence Howard, and Danny Strong's motion for attorney's fees is granted in part. Defendants are awarded attorney's fees against plaintiff Ron Newt in the amount of $48,000. No opposition filed. Per Tamkin v. CBS Broadcasting, Inc. (2011) 193 Cal.App. 4th 133, at least several causes of action in Mr. Newt's first amended complaint arise from activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute because the creation and airing of the television show Empire constitutes protected activity under that statute. Defendants' request for $117,156.50 (amount requested) in attorney's fees is excessive given the issues involved in this motion and their anti-SLAPP motion. While the court does not question that counsel for defendants spent the hours that they say they spent and their clients are permitted to have them spend those hours, the hours spent far exceed what was reasonably necessary to succeed on the motions. The court concludes that 2 weeks of work (80 hours) at an hourly rate of $600 is a reasonable amount of hours to spend on these motions. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

The matter is recalled because of a late appearance on behalf of plaintiff. The Court hears from both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court notes that plaintiff, Mr. Newt is not self-represented. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)
